metadata.rationale: Die sportlichen Basisangaben zum 1:1 und zu Polzins 50. Spiel haben einen realen faktischen Kern. Irreführend ist jedoch, dass bearbeitete Drittgrafiken als offizielle Spieltags- bzw. Stadiongrafiken gerahmt und damit in einen falschen Herkunfts- und Nutzungskontext gesetzt werden.

best_trajectory.observation:
The image appears to be a **designed broadcast/league graphic**, not a natural photograph—so it is inherently “edited” in the sense of being a **digital scoreboard overlay**.

**Signs consistent with an authentic graphic (not AI-generated):**
- Clean, symmetrical layout with consistent typography and spacing across all elements.
- Logos (HSV diamond and 1. FC Köln crest with goat) look crisp and proportionally correct, without the common AI artifacts (warped shapes, inconsistent outlines).
- Text is legible and coherent throughout (“BUNDESLIGA | 11. SPIELTAG”, “VOLKSPARKSTADION | HAMBURG”, “ENDSTAND”, scorers/minutes, “ZUSCHAUER 57.000”, “SPORT1”, “BUNDESLIGA.COM”) with no garbling.
- Lighting/background stadium blur looks like a standard composited backdrop used in sports graphics; no obvious mismatched shadows or broken perspective.

**Possible manipulation / synthetic concerns:**
- No clear visual evidence of AI synthesis (no distorted letters, inconsistent iconography, or impossible geometry).
- No obvious copy-paste seams or inconsistent compression around specific text blocks; everything shares the same polished design style.

**Conclusion:** This looks like a **legitimate, professionally produced match result graphic** (digital by nature). I don’t see strong indicators of AI generation or tampering *within the image itself*.
